name: Lyle Mays
abstract: Lyle David Mays (November 27, 1953 – February 10, 2020) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and member of the Pat Metheny Group. Metheny and Mays composed and arranged nearly all of the group's music, for which Mays won eleven Grammy Awards.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Bobby McFerrin & Friends, plus The New York Giants

about_quotes.speaker: Aebersold Jamey
about_quotes.evidence: I’m pretty sure that was the camp that Pat Metheny came to, Lyle Mays on piano and Steve Rodby on bass. They met for the first time, and after that they got together and formed the Pat Metheny Group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Aebersold Jamey — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: we were a force... we went to see Pat Metheny who we were friends with, and he just got his record deal with ECM with Lyle Mays and that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 7, 2023
